Notes: WARNING - this is the user-flagged ambiguity. Heiss correctly separates 'Temporal lobe' from 'Hippocampus' and 'Insular lobe' (they are distinct rows). Siletti groups: temporal-cortex Brodmann/gyral regions belong here ONLY. Do NOT include Hippocampus, Insular, or Parahippocampal (LEC, MEC, A35-36, A35r, TH-TL) when matching to Heiss Temporal lobe value. Parahippocampal gyrus is debated as temporal-lobe member.

Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human FI; Human Idg; Human Ig
Notes: Heiss correctly treats insula as its own lobe (it sits between frontal/parietal/temporal). Siletti splits insula by cytoarchitecture: FI (agranular), Idg (dysgranular), Ig (granular). Direct many-to-one match. DO NOT also count Insula values under Frontal/Temporal/Parietal lobe.

Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human CA1R-CA2R; Human CA1R-CA2R-CA3R; Human CA3R; Human DGR-CA4Rpy; Human Sub; Human CA1U; Human CA1-3; Human CA1U-CA2U-CA3U; Human CA2U-CA3U; Human DGU-CA4Upy; Human CA1C-CA3C; Human CA4C-DGC; Human Sub (tail)
Notes: Heiss correctly separates Hippocampus from Temporal lobe. Hippocampus is allocortex (3-layer), NOT neocortex - it should never be inside the Temporal-lobe rate. Siletti has 13 subfield rows (head/body/tail x CA1/CA2/CA3/CA4/DG/Sub) all aggregating to whole hippocampus. Note: entorhinal cortex (LEC, MEC) is technically parahippocampal allocortex - belongs with hippocampus formation, not temporal neocortex. Heiss does not separately list entorhinal.
